era: 1994-present. Post-Grunge. E=7. Seattle (formed), Los Angeles (relocated). Roswell/Capitol Records to RCA. Dave Grohl: post-Nirvana formation, solo project that became a band. 11 studio albums. One of the most commercially successful rock bands of the 1990s-2020s.
members: Dave Grohl (vocals/guitar, born January 14 1969 Warren OH; Nirvana drummer 1990-1994)
Nate Mendel (bass, born December 2 1968 Seattle WA, from 1995)
Taylor Hawkins (drums, born February 17 1972 Fort Worth TX, died March 25 2022 Bogotá Colombia, age 50)
Pat Smear (guitar, born August 5 1959 Los Angeles CA, from 1994/rejoined 2000/2011)
William Goldsmith (drums, 1994-1997, Sunny Day Real Estate)
key_albums: Foo Fighters (1995, Roswell/Capitol) -- debut; Grohl solo recording; This Is a Call, I'll Stick Around, Big Me
The Colour and the Shape (1997, Roswell/Capitol) -- breakout; Everlong, Monkey Wrench, My Hero; Taylor Hawkins first full recording
There Is Nothing Left to Lose (1999, Roswell/Capitol) -- Gimme Stitches, Stacked Actors, Aurora; Grammy Best Rock Album
One by One (2002, Roswell/Capitol) -- All My Life, Times Like These, Have It All
In Your Honor (2005, Roswell/Capitol) -- double album; Best of You
Echoes Silence Patience & Grace (2007, RCA) -- The Pretender, Long Road to Ruin; 2 Grammys
Wasting Light (2011, RCA) -- recorded in Grohl's garage; Walk, Rope, Bridge Burning; 4 Grammys
Concrete and Gold (2017, RCA) -- Run, The Sky Is a Neighborhood
Medicine at Midnight (2021, RCA) -- Making a Fire, Shame Shame
But Here We Are (2023, RCA) -- Grohl grief response to mother's death; Rescued, The Teacher

theory_nodes.concept: NIRVANA GENEALOGY. Grohl was Nirvana's drummer October 1990 through Cobain's death April 5 1994. Nevermind (1991): Grohl's drumming is the physical foundation of the album's commercial power (Smells Like Teen Spirit groove, In Bloom's dynamics). Foo Fighters formed within weeks of Cobain's death as Grohl's response to loss through continued work. The formation date (1994) places Foo Fighters as the first post-Nirvana major act from that lineage.
theory_nodes.description: Grohl's Nirvana tenure and Foo Fighters as the direct post-Nirvana formation.

theory_nodes.concept: E=7 CALIBRATION. Post-Grunge: melodic accessibility prioritized over sonic extremity. The Foo Fighters' power comes from hooks and emotional directness rather than from the dissonant harmonic weight (AIC, 313), sonic density (Soundgarden, 312), or emotional devastation (Nirvana, 311) of the E=8-9 Summit acts. Everlong (1997) is the best calibration example: E=7 emotional commitment, E=7 melodic force -- not Summit territory, but the most durable melodic rock output of the grunge generation.
theory_nodes.description: E=7 as post-grunge calibration: melodic accessibility at high quality as the energy register.

theory_nodes.concept: Everlong (The Colour and the Shape, 1997): open-D tuning, alternate picking pattern, Grohl's most melodically complete vocal, Hawkins' drumming as a structural event in the song rather than accompaniment. The acoustic MTV Unplugged version (1997) demonstrates the song survives its production -- it is not dependent on volume. The song is the engine's strongest E=7 melodic achievement in the grunge zone.
theory_nodes.description: Everlong as the definitive Foo Fighters document and the engine's strongest E=7 melodic achievement.

theory_nodes.concept: TAYLOR HAWKINS (February 17 1972 - March 25 2022). Died Bogotá, Colombia, age 50, before scheduled Estéreo Picnic festival performance. Joined Foo Fighters 1997 after stint with Alanis Morissette. His drumming: power plus precision, emotional commitment matching Grohl's vocal commitments note-for-note at the rhythm layer. But Here We Are (2023) dedicated in part to Hawkins. The engine documents his death with factual respect.
theory_nodes.description: Taylor Hawkins: biography, contribution, and death documented with factual respect.

theory_nodes.concept: WASTING LIGHT (2011): recorded in Grohl's Encino garage on analog tape. Butch Vig producing (Vig produced Nevermind). Krist Novoselic played on White Limo. Four Grammys including Best Rock Album. The garage recording concept is significant: Grohl chose to reduce production mediation to make the most direct-feeling Foo Fighters record. The Nevermind connection (Vig) and the analog method are both deliberate acts of connection to the source.
theory_nodes.description: Wasting Light's garage analog recording as a deliberate act of connection to the Nevermind lineage.

theory_nodes.concept: PAT SMEAR: born 1959, Germs guitarist, first wave Los Angeles punk. Grohl recruited Smear as second guitarist 1994 -- the first post-Nirvana move was to bring in a punk elder. The Germs (degree-parallel: LA punk 1977-1980) connect through Smear to the Foo Fighters. The Seattle act's first addition is an LA punk figure: geography collapses through personnel.
theory_nodes.description: Pat Smear as the Germs-to-Foo-Fighters punk genealogy thread.

cross_references.relationship: Grohl was Nirvana's drummer through Nevermind and In Utero. Foo Fighters formed April-June 1994, weeks after Cobain's death. The most direct genealogical connection in the post-Summit sequence.

Consecutive post-Summit entries. Pearl Jam E=8 (continued operation, commercial persistence). Foo Fighters E=7 (post-grunge accessibility, commercial persistence through different mechanism). Both the most sustained operations of their respective grunge/post-grunge registers.

cross_references.artist: Sunny Day Real Estate / William Goldsmith
cross_references.relationship: William Goldsmith (original Foo Fighters drummer, 1994-1997) from Sunny Day Real Estate. Post-grunge genealogy runs through Foo Fighters to Seattle emo territory.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=7 + E=7 = E=14. Post-Summit gradient continues. Foo Fighters E=7 (post-grunge melodic commitment, emotional directness without Summit extremity) paired with Varied Thrush in fog E=7 (a single sustained haunting note in reduced visibility, emotional directness without maximum acoustic volume). Both E=7 through focused singular quality rather than collective density or individual extremity.
